_comment: CNA campaign victory geography (rule 64.7). Hex labels transcribed from docs/rules/90 'SUMMARY OF IMPORTANT LOCATIONS', cross-validated against scenario-setup rules 60/61/62 and the engine/OOB constants. VP values are the rule-64.73 Geographic Occupation Point table. Consumed by game.campaign_victory.
_supply_sources_note: The two harbours rules 64.71 and 64.72 name: a Supply Dump must be feedable from one of them 'in any way' for a combat unit's truck-MP line of supply to it to count. Consumed by game.campaign_victory.CampaignVictory.supply_sources; a null source has no hex on this map and is absent from the trace.
_tripoli_note: Tripoli is off-map (8.81: the Tripoli/Tunisia boxes sit on the western edge of Map A; 8.88 makes them Supply Dumps of unlimited capacity; 22.31 names the Tripoli Box). Its ON-MAP GATEWAY IS HEX A2802, and that is the rulebook's own number, not a reading: 8.85 says 'For a unit to be moved off the game map towards Tripolitania it must start that Operations Stage in hex A2802', and A2802 is one step from Nofilia (A2703) along the coast road. IT IS NULL BECAUSE OUR MAP HAS A2802 AS SEA. data/terrain_A.json transcribes A2802 -- and A1816, El Agheila, which 61.43C names as a road hex -- as sea: the Gulf of Sirte coastline is colour-sampled roughly one hex too far inland along that stretch. game.scenario.campaign already patches this class of defect where a piece stands on the hex ('coastal ports colour-sample as sea'); A2802 has no piece on it. FIX THE COASTLINE, THEN SET THIS TO A2802. Until then the 64.71/64.72 trace runs on Tobruk alone -- which only makes 64.71 (the Axis auto-win) stricter than the book, but would make 64.72 (the Commonwealth auto-win at Game-Turn 35) fire off a sampling error the moment the Axis loses Tobruk. See game.campaign_victory's module docstring.
